latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is my understanding with clear scope and defaults; if this aligns, I’m ready to proceed to spec.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: Augment Rivvy Portal sign-in so that all failed or blocked attempts are handled enumeration-safely and a temporary lockout is enforced after repeated failures. The Login screen (/login) calls the Portal API (Rivvy Portal Services) to authenticate email/password. Regardless of cause (unknown email, wrong password, disabled, invited, or currently locked), the client receives the same generic error while the service records the precise reason internally. A per-account lockout triggers after too many recent failed attempts and auto-expires. Successful authentication resets failure counters. If the user selects Remember me, the authenticated session uses an extended inactivity timeout, persisting across browser restarts, while the default 8-hour inactivity expiry remains for non-Remember me sessions. Disabled accounts are denied sign-in (with generic messaging) without surfacing their status. This story only covers respecting disabled state at login; it does not include any admin/UX to disable or manage accounts (handled by a separate item).

in: Enumeration-safe handling of all failed/blocked sign-in attempts via Portal API with a single generic error surfaced on the Login screen
Per-account temporary lockout after repeated failures within a time window, with automatic unlock after a duration
Reset/clear failure counters and lockout state upon successful sign-in
Respect UserAccount status at login: deny sign-in for disabled or invited accounts with generic error
Optional Remember me to extend session inactivity timeout beyond the default
Server-side logging/metrics of exact failure reasons and lockout events (not exposed to the client)
out: Creating, editing, or displaying disabled accounts (covered by sibling 'Disabled account handling' work item)
Post-login routing/destination logic (covered by separate feature)
Password reset/forgot password or onboarding for invited users
MFA/2FA, CAPTCHA, or IP/device/global rate limiting
Admin UI/APIs to manually manage lockouts or user states

assumptions: UserAccount.status values active|invited|disabled (in user_account) are authoritative for sign-in eligibility.
Generic error copy for all failures/blocks: "We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password."
Lockout policy defaults: threshold=5 failed attempts within 15 minutes leads to a 15-minute account lockout.
Lockout is enforced per account (not per IP/device) and automatically clears after the lockout duration; counters reset on successful sign-in.
Remember me extends the inactivity timeout to 30 days (sliding on activity) and persists across browser restarts; non-Remember me remains 8 hours inactivity.
API returns HTTP 401 with the same generic error for all failures, including when locked, to avoid enumeration.
Whitespace trimming and case-insensitive email comparison are already handled by the completed valid sign-in story and are unchanged here.
The existing password verification routine (from the completed sign-in story) remains the source of truth; no changes to password storage are required.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an unknown email, wrong password, disabled, invited, or locked account, when I attempt to sign in via /login, then the response is HTTP 401 and the UI shows the same generic error text with no account state revealed.

Given I fail to sign in 5 times within 15 minutes on an active account, when I attempt again before 15 minutes elapse since lockout start, then sign-in is blocked and I still see the same generic error.
Given my account is locked, when 15 minutes have elapsed since lockout start, then I can attempt sign-in again and the attempt is evaluated normally.
Given I successfully sign in, then any prior failed-attempt counters and lockout state are cleared/reset and lastLoginAt is updated.
Given I sign in successfully without Remember me, then my session expires after 8 hours of inactivity (unchanged).
Given I sign in successfully with Remember me selected, then my session persists across browser restarts and expires after 30 days of inactivity unless I log out.

Given any failed sign-in attempt or a lockout event, then Rivvy Portal Services logs the specific reason and relevant metadata (e.g., timestamp and user/email identifier), while the client only receives the generic error.
Given a disabled or invited account attempts to sign in, then authentication is denied with HTTP 401 and the same generic error; no status-specific messaging is presented.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Blocked and failed sign-in handling (enumeration-safe, lockout, disabled)

increments.title: Enumeration-safe failures, per-account lockout, disabled respect, and Remember me session extension
increments.intent:
- Backend (Rivvy Portal Services, Portal API)
  - Configuration (env/feature flags with sane defaults):
    - AUTH_FAILED_THRESHOLD=5
    - AUTH_FAILED_WINDOW_MIN=15
    - AUTH_LOCKOUT_MIN=15
    - AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS=30
    - SESSION_DEFAULT_IDLE_HOURS remains 8 (existing)
  - Data persistence (rivvy_portal.user_account) migration:
    - Add columns: failed_signin_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0; last_failed_signin_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL; lockout_until TIMESTAMPTZ NULL.
    - No backfill required; defaults cover existing rows. Provide down migration to drop columns.
  - Login endpoint logic (augment existing email/password handler):
    - Normalize email (trim, lowercase) per existing behavior; lookup by CITEXT email.
    - If account exists and lockout_until > now: log reason=LOCKED_OUT; return HTTP 401 with generic message; skip password check.
    - If account not found: run a dummy constant-time password verification against a static hash to mitigate timing side-channels; log reason=UNKNOWN_EMAIL; return HTTP 401 with generic message.
    - If account found and status != 'active' (invited|disabled): treat as a failed attempt for counter purposes, update counters (see Failure handling), log reason=STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE with status; return HTTP 401 with generic message.
    - If account active: verify password via existing constant-time routine.
      - On success: within a transaction, reset failed_signin_count=0, last_failed_signin_at=NULL, lockout_until=NULL; set last_login_at=now; issue session (see Session issuance); respond 200 as per existing contract.
      - On failure: update counters (see Failure handling); respond HTTP 401 with generic message.
  - Failure handling (transactional and race-safe):
    - SELECT ... FOR UPDATE the user row; compute window and count updates server-side.
    - If last_failed_signin_at IS NULL or older than AUTH_FAILED_WINDOW_MIN minutes: set failed_signin_count=1, last_failed_signin_at=now.
    - Else increment failed_signin_count by 1.
    - If failed_signin_count >= AUTH_FAILED_THRESHOLD within window: set lockout_until = now + AUTH_LOCKOUT_MIN minutes; set failed_signin_count=0; set last_failed_signin_at=NULL.
  - Auto-unlock behavior:
    - No background job required. Presence of lockout_until > now blocks login; when now >= lockout_until, attempts proceed normally. Successful sign-in clears fields; a new failure restarts counters/window.
  - Session issuance (Remember me):
    - Extend existing session creation to accept rememberMe boolean from client.
    - If rememberMe=true: set server-side session idle timeout to AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS (sliding) and cookie Max-Age accordingly; ensure HttpOnly, Secure, and current SameSite policy.
    - If rememberMe=false: retain existing 8-hour inactivity timeout (session cookie or 8h Max-Age per current implementation).
  - Logging/metrics (no enumeration leakage):
    - Log auth_attempt events with: userId (if known) or normalized email, reason in {SUCCESS, UNKNOWN_EMAIL, BAD_PASSWORD, STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE, LOCKED_OUT}, failed_count, lockout_until, ip (if available), timestamp.
    - Emit counters: auth.success_total, auth.failures_total{reason}, auth.lockouts_total.
  - API response semantics:
    - All failures return HTTP 401 with { message: "We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password." }.
    - Success unchanged from completed sign-in story.
- Frontend (Rivvy Portal UI, /login):
  - Add Remember me checkbox (unchecked by default) and include rememberMe in the login API request body.
  - On any 401: display the single generic error; do not vary copy or show lockout hints/countdowns.
  - Keep all other behaviors and post-login routing unchanged (handled by separate feature).
- Security considerations:
  - Use constant-time password checks; perform dummy verify for unknown emails to reduce timing differences.
  - Avoid status-specific errors or varied HTTP codes for failures to maintain enumeration safety.
  - Ensure cookies are HttpOnly, Secure; preserve SameSite policy (Lax or Strict per current standard).
- Testing strategy:
  - Unit (service): counter increment/reset; windowing and lockout_until setting; auto-unlock path; status ineligible handling; unknown-email dummy verify; rememberMe TTL selection.
  - Integration (API): repeated failures lead to lockout; lockout blocks with 401; post-lockout attempts proceed; success clears counters and sets last_login_at; responses always generic on failure.
  - UI/E2E: Remember me persists session across browser restarts within TTL; non-Remember me expires after 8h inactivity; UI shows same error for all 401s.
- Observability/ops:
  - Document new env vars; ensure they are configurable per environment; add basic logs for monitoring. Dashboards/alerts are optional and out of scope.
- Backward compatibility:
  - Successful sign-in flow remains unchanged aside from optional rememberMe; new DB columns are additive with safe defaults.

messages.content: Welcome! We’re set to work on “Blocked and failed sign-in handling (enumeration-safe, lockout, disabled).” I have the product backlog and full architecture context available. Are there any specific architecture entities or diagrams you’d like to highlight as most relevant (e.g., Portal API, user_account table, Login screen)?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
I couldn't parse the structured response. Please try again.
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here is my understanding with clear scope and defaults; if this aligns, I’m ready to proceed to spec.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software developer to review this.
Now let's get the Test Engineer to review this and define the test plan...
Unit tests cover lockout window logic, enumeration-safe responses, session TTL selection, and logging; functional tests validate Login UI error messaging and Remember me behavior with mocked API.

latestTestPlannerResponse.message: Unit tests cover lockout window logic, enumeration-safe responses, session TTL selection, and logging; functional tests validate Login UI error messaging and Remember me behavior with mocked API.

testPlan.title: Service: unknown email returns 401 with generic message, dummy verify executed, logs UNKNOWN_EMAIL
testPlan.description: Given a non-existent email, when authenticate(email, password) is called, then the service performs a dummy constant-time password verification, logs reason=UNKNOWN_EMAIL with email and timestamp, and returns an error mapped to HTTP 401 with message 'We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password.'

testPlan.title: Service: wrong password increments counter, logs BAD_PASSWORD, returns 401 with generic message
testPlan.description: Given an active account and an incorrect password within the failure window, when authenticate is called, then failed_signin_count increments, last_failed_signin_at updates, reason=BAD_PASSWORD is logged with metadata, and an error mapped to HTTP 401 with the generic message is returned.

testPlan.title: Service: disabled account denied, counts as failure, logs STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE, returns 401 generic
testPlan.description: Given a user with status=disabled, when authenticate is called, then the attempt is treated as a failure for counter purposes, counters update per window rules, reason=STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE with status=disabled is logged, and an error mapped to HTTP 401 with the generic message is returned without revealing status.

testPlan.title: Service: invited account denied, counts as failure, logs STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE, returns 401 generic
testPlan.description: Given a user with status=invited, when authenticate is called, then the attempt increments failure counters per window rules, logs reason=STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE with status=invited, and returns an error mapped to HTTP 401 with the generic message with no account state revealed.

testPlan.title: Service: locked-out account short-circuits with 401 generic and no password check; logs LOCKED_OUT
testPlan.description: Given lockout_until > now for an account, when authenticate is called, then password verification is skipped, reason=LOCKED_OUT is logged, and an error mapped to HTTP 401 with the same generic message is returned.

testPlan.title: Failure handling: increment counters within window below threshold
testPlan.description: Given last_failed_signin_at is within AUTH_FAILED_WINDOW_MIN and failed_signin_count < AUTH_FAILED_THRESHOLD-1, when a new failure occurs, then failed_signin_count increments by 1 and last_failed_signin_at is updated to now; lockout_until remains NULL.

testPlan.title: Failure handling: reaching threshold sets lockout_until and resets counters
testPlan.description: Given failures within AUTH_FAILED_WINDOW_MIN reach AUTH_FAILED_THRESHOLD, when the latest failure is processed, then lockout_until is set to now + AUTH_LOCKOUT_MIN, failed_signin_count resets to 0, and last_failed_signin_at is cleared.

testPlan.title: Failure handling: window reset after inactivity
testPlan.description: Given last_failed_signin_at is older than AUTH_FAILED_WINDOW_MIN, when a new failure occurs, then failed_signin_count is reset to 1 and last_failed_signin_at is set to now (no immediate lockout).

testPlan.title: Auto-unlock: attempts after lockout_until are evaluated normally
testPlan.description: Given lockout_until <= now, when authenticate is called, then the service proceeds to evaluate status/password normally (no lockout short-circuit) and does not re-lock unless new failures recur.

testPlan.title: Success path: counters and lockout cleared; lastLoginAt updated
testPlan.description: Given prior failures and/or a past lockout, when correct credentials are provided, then failed_signin_count=0, last_failed_signin_at=NULL, lockout_until=NULL, and last_login_at is updated to now.

testPlan.title: Controller: all failure causes map to HTTP 401 with identical generic message
testPlan.description: Given service errors for UNKNOWN_EMAIL, BAD_PASSWORD, STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE, or LOCKED_OUT, when the controller handles /login, then the response status is 401 and the JSON body contains the exact message 'We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password.' for each case.

testPlan.title: Controller: failure response contains only generic message (no state details)
testPlan.description: Given any failed sign-in, when the controller responds, then the JSON body includes only a 'message' field (no status, reason, lockout data, or user hints).

testPlan.title: Session issuance: rememberMe=true sets 30-day idle timeout and persistent cookie
testPlan.description: Given rememberMe=true at login, when a session is issued, then the server-side idle TTL is AUTH_REMEMBER_TTL_DAYS (default 30 days, sliding) and the cookie has Max-Age for 30 days with HttpOnly and Secure set and SameSite per configured policy.

testPlan.title: Session issuance: rememberMe=false uses default 8-hour idle timeout
testPlan.description: Given rememberMe is not selected, when a session is issued, then the server idle TTL remains 8 hours and the cookie is a session cookie or has Max-Age for 8 hours per current implementation.

testPlan.title: Audit logging: failure reasons and metadata are recorded
testPlan.description: Given any failed sign-in or lockout event, when processed, then an audit log entry is emitted with reason (e.g., UNKNOWN_EMAIL, BAD_PASSWORD, STATUS_NOT_ELIGIBLE, LOCKED_OUT), userId or normalized email, failed_count, lockout_until (if applicable), ip (if available), and timestamp.

testPlan.title: Login UI: shows generic error on API 401 (unknown email)
testPlan.description: Given the Login form and MSW returns 401 with the generic message for an unknown email attempt, when the user submits, then the UI displays exactly 'We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password.' and no additional hints.

testPlan.title: Login UI: shows same generic error on API 401 when account is locked
testPlan.description: Given MSW returns 401 for a locked account attempt, when the user submits, then the UI displays the same generic error text and does not show countdowns or status-specific messaging.

testPlan.title: Login UI: Remember me checkbox is unchecked by default
testPlan.description: Given the /login screen renders, then the Remember me checkbox is not selected until the user explicitly checks it.

testPlan.title: Login UI: request includes rememberMe=true when checkbox selected
testPlan.description: Given the user checks Remember me and submits valid credentials, when the login request is sent, then the payload contains rememberMe=true; when unchecked, rememberMe is false or omitted per client contract.

testPlan.title: Login UI: clears error on subsequent successful sign-in
testPlan.description: Given a prior 401 displayed the generic error, when the next sign-in succeeds (MSW returns 200), then the error message is removed from the UI.

testPlan.title: Remember me persists session across page reload in mocked environment
testPlan.description: Given MSW simulates setting a persistent auth cookie on success with rememberMe=true, when the test simulates a page reload, then the app initializes as authenticated (e.g., no redirect back to /login), indicating persistence across restarts.

testPlan.title: Non-Remember me session does not persist across page reload in mocked environment
testPlan.description: Given MSW simulates a session cookie on success with rememberMe=false, when the test simulates a page reload, then the app initializes unauthenticated (e.g., shows /login), indicating non-persistent default behavior.

specIntentTexts.INC-1:
/shape-spec ## Feature Description
Augment Rivvy Portal sign-in so that all failed or blocked attempts are handled enumeration-safely and a temporary lockout is enforced after repeated failures. The Login screen (/login) calls the Portal API (Rivvy Portal Services) to authenticate email/password. Regardless of cause (unknown email, wrong password, disabled, invited, or currently locked), the client receives the same generic error while the service records the precise reason internally. A per-account lockout triggers after too many recent failed attempts and auto-expires. Successful authentication resets failure counters. If the user selects Remember me, the authenticated session uses an extended inactivity timeout, persisting across browser restarts, while the default 8-hour inactivity expiry remains for non-Remember me sessions. Disabled accounts are denied sign-in (with generic messaging) without surfacing their status. This story only covers respecting disabled state at login; it does not include any admin/UX to disable or manage accounts (handled by a separate item).

## In Scope
- Enumeration-safe handling of all failed/blocked sign-in attempts via Portal API with a single generic error surfaced on the Login screen
- Per-account temporary lockout after repeated failures within a time window, with automatic unlock after a duration
- Reset/clear failure counters and lockout state upon successful sign-in
- Respect UserAccount status at login: deny sign-in for disabled or invited accounts with generic error
- Optional Remember me to extend session inactivity timeout beyond the default
- Server-side logging/metrics of exact failure reasons and lockout events (not exposed to the client)

## Out of Scope
- Creating, editing, or displaying disabled accounts (covered by sibling 'Disabled account handling' work item)
- Post-login routing/destination logic (covered by separate feature)
- Password reset/forgot password or onboarding for invited users
- MFA/2FA, CAPTCHA, or IP/device/global rate limiting
- SSO/OIDC flows and providers
- Admin UI/APIs to manually manage lockouts or user states

## Sibling Items (handled separately — do NOT implement these)
- [COMPLETED] Sign in with valid credentials: As a user, I can successfully authenticate with a valid email and password, resulting in an authenticated session; post-authentication destination is handled by the role-based routing feature.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given a valid, active user account with the correct email and password, when I submit the login form, then an authenticated session is established.
- Given I sign in successfully without selecting Remember me, when I am inactive for 8 consecutive hours, then my session expires and I must sign in again.
- Given I enter an email with leading or trailing whitespace, when I attempt to sign in, then the whitespace is ignored and the trimmed value is validated.
- Given I enter an email using different letter casing than stored, when I attempt to sign in, then the comparison is case-insensitive and I can still sign in if the credentials are correct.
- Post-authentication destination is handled by the Post-Login Role & Organization Routing feature and validated by its acceptance criteria.
- [IN_PROGRESS] Disabled account handling

## Assumptions
- UserAccount.status values active|invited|disabled (in user_account) are authoritative for sign-in eligibility.
- Generic error copy for all failures/blocks: "We couldn’t sign you in. Check your email and password."
- Lockout policy defaults: threshold=5 failed attempts within 15 minutes leads to a 15-minute account lockout.
- Lockout is enforced per account (not per IP/device) and automatically clears after the lockout duration; counters reset on successful sign-in.
- Remember me extends the inactivity timeout to 30 days (sliding on activity) and persists across browser restarts; non-Remember me remains 8 hours inactivity.
- API returns HTTP 401 with the same generic error for all failures, including when locked, to avoid enumeration.
- Whitespace trimming and case-insensitive email comparison are already handled by the completed valid sign-in story and are unchanged here.
- The existing password verification routine (from the completed sign-in story) remains the source of truth; no changes to password storage are required.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given an unknown email, wrong password, disabled, invited, or locked account, when I attempt to sign in via /login, then the response is HTTP 401 and the UI shows the same generic error text with no account state revealed.
- Given I fail to sign in 5 times within 15 minutes on an active account, when I attempt again before 15 minutes elapse since lockout start, then sign-in is blocked and I still see the same generic error.
- Given my account is locked, when 15 minutes have elapsed since lockout start, then I can attempt sign-in again and the attempt is evaluated normally.
- Given I successfully sign in, then any prior failed-attempt counters and lockout state are cleared/reset and lastLoginAt is updated.
- Given I sign in successfully without Remember me, then my session expires after 8 hours of inactivity (unchanged).
- Given I sign in successfully with Remember me selected, then my session persists across browser restarts and expires after 30 days of inactivity unless I log out.
- Given any failed sign-in attempt or a lockout event, then Rivvy Portal Services logs the specific reason and relevant metadata (e.g., timestamp and user/email identifier), while the client only receives the generic error.
- Given a disabled or invited account attempts to sign in, then authentication is denied with HTTP 401 and the same generic error; no status-specific messaging is presented.

# Architecture Meta-Model Reference

This section describes the entity types and relationship types in the Architecture Meta-Model. Use this reference to understand the structure of the architecture model data that follows.

> **Note:** `ui_contracts` is excluded from the model data below. It is a deprecated duplicate of `interfaces` and is pending removal.

## Entity Types

### Application Architecture

- **`applications`** -- Top-level deployable systems
- **`app_components`** -- Human-defined grouping of service types/tiers/layers within an application on a technical basis (e.g. "Web Tier", "Persistence Tier"), so that certain important things can be stated about this grouping e.g. "our company uses React/TS for all frontends" + if a project has 3 services whose parent app_component is "UI Tier", then those 3 deployed services should be React/TS according to company technical standards.
- **`services`** -- Runtime components within an application
- **`interfaces`** -- API boundaries exposed by services
- **`endpoints`** -- Individual operations on an interface
- **`application_points`** -- **Polymorphic wrapper (1:1 with a concrete entity)**. Every application_point row maps to exactly one concrete entity (application, app_component, service, or interface). This mechanism allows relationships like `data_movements` to flexibly reference any level of the application architecture as source/target. **AUTO-MANAGED by the backend -- the LLM must NEVER create these directly.**

### Code-Level Entities

- **`classes`** -- Code-level entities. E.g. if Java, it's a Java class. Used in UML "Class diagrams".
- **`methods`** -- Code-level methods on classes. Used in "Class diagrams" and referenced in other diagrams e.g. a sequence diagram might show an internal calculation `calcMyNumbers(...)`.

### Data Entities

- **`logical_data_entities`** -- Conceptual data objects (DTOs), often representing the actual shape of data transferring between applications in APIs.
- **`logical_data_attributes`** -- Fields on logical data entities (name, dataType, isPrimaryKey, isNullable).
- **`physical_data_entities`** -- Persisted storage structures (database tables, collections, physical files).
- **`physical_data_attributes`** -- Columns on physical data entities (name, dataType, isPrimaryKey, isNullable).
- **`data_entity_points`** -- **Polymorphic wrapper (1:1 with a concrete entity)**. Every data_entity_point maps to exactly one logical or physical data entity. Allows relationships to flexibly reference either type. **AUTO-MANAGED by the backend -- the LLM must NEVER create these directly.**

### Business Domain

- **`business_users`** -- Actors/roles who use the system.
- **`business_processes`** -- High-level business workflows.
- **`process_activities`** -- Steps within a business process.
- **`business_points`** -- **Polymorphic wrapper (1:1 with a concrete entity)**. Every business_point maps to either a `business_process` or an `activity`. Allows relationships to reference either a very high-level business process or a medium-to-high level business activity. **AUTO-MANAGED by the backend -- the LLM must NEVER create these directly.**
- **`app_business_points`** -- **Super-polymorphic wrapper**. Allows the flexible choice of either an `application_point` or a `business_point` in a relationship. **AUTO-MANAGED by the backend -- the LLM must NEVER create these directly.**
- **`interactions`** -- A flexible definition allowing you to define any kind of interaction that a user may have with `application_points` or `business_points` (hence the use of `app_business_points`). Architecturally loose by design.

### State Machine

- **`events`** -- Events in a state machine model.
- **`states`** -- States in a state machine model.
- **`state_transitions`** -- Transitions between states in a state machine.

### Activity/Workflow

- **`activities`** -- Steps in an activity/workflow diagram.
- **`activity_flows`** -- Connections between activities in a workflow.
- **`activity_partitions`** -- Swimlanes/groupings in activity diagrams.

### UI Architecture

- **`ui_screens`** -- Frontend pages/views. Key attributes: **`route`** (the URL path pattern this screen is served at, e.g. `/dashboard`, `/projects/:id/settings`), **`description`** (purpose and content of the screen). When the architecture model includes UI screens, their `route` attribute tells you the exact URL path for navigation.
- **`ui_components`** -- Component/element within a screen (not a UI widget). What would be defined in multiple frontend frameworks in code in `src/components`. Components build on top of each other e.g. basic components are used in the definition of parent, more complex components. Key attributes: **`description`** (what the component renders/does).
- **`ui_actions`** -- User-triggered or system-triggered actions in UI. Key attributes: **`description`** (what happens when the action fires), **`trigger`** (what initiates the action, e.g. button click, form submit).
- **`ui_characteristics`** -- Business features / UX characteristics linked to services that are "UI tier" i.e. frontend UIs.

### Business Logic & Packaging

- **`business_logics`** -- Business rules/domain logic owned by a service.
- **`package_sets`** -- A set of "packages" that allows someone to state their full src directory/package structure.
- **`packages`** -- Code packaging (think Java Package, albeit this word is different in other languages e.g. Python Module).
- **`package_set_default_rules`** -- Auto-resolution rules for package set selection.

## Relationship Types

- **`business_user_business_points`** -- Links business users to business points (polymorphic: business processes or activities).
- **`application_point_business_points`** -- Links application points to business points.
- **`logical_data_entity_relationships`** -- Links between logical data entities (e.g. FK-style references, associations).
- **`logical_data_entity_physical_data_entities`** -- Maps logical entities to physical entities (logical<->physical mapping).
- **`logical_data_attribute_physical_data_attributes`** -- Maps logical attributes to physical attributes.
- **`data_movements`** -- Data flowing between application points, optionally referencing a data entity point or interface.
- **`interface_logical_entities`** -- Links interfaces to the logical entities they expose.
- **`ui_workflow_transitions`** -- Navigation/flow between UI screens.
- **`application_point_business_logics`** -- Links application points to business logic rules.

## Important Notes on Polymorphic Wrappers

The following entity types are **polymorphic wrappers** that are **automatically managed by the backend**. The LLM must **NEVER** create, update, or delete these entities directly. They are created and maintained automatically when their corresponding concrete entities are saved:

1. **`application_points`** -- Auto-created for applications, app_components, services, and interfaces
2. **`data_entity_points`** -- Auto-created for logical_data_entities and physical_data_entities
3. **`business_points`** -- Auto-created for business_processes and activities
4. **`app_business_points`** -- Auto-created to bridge application_points and business_points


## Architecture Context
Here is the part of the architecture that directly applies to this spec and it must be reviewed as part of the spec above. It is expressed as a JSON payload about business, application, data, behavioural and/or UI architecture:

{"entities":[{"id":1,"name":"RS UI Tier","type":"appComponents","fields":{"applicationId":"app-mlxwja0d-7swkq"}},{"id":2,"name":"RS Service Tier","type":"appComponents","fields":{"applicationId":"app-mlxwja0d-7swkq"}},{"id":3,"name":"RS Persistence Tier","type":"appComponents","fields":{"applicationId":"app-mlxwja0d-7swkq"}},{"id":4,"name":"AWS
